body.text: **Top story:** Pentagon designates Anthropic a national security supply-chain risk — an unprecedented move against an American AI company — while Apple holds its biggest product launch of the year.

items.text: **🚨 1. Pentagon Designates Anthropic a National Security Risk**

body.text:
The DoD formally labeled Anthropic a **"supply-chain risk to national security"** — the first American company ever designated as such. The Pentagon demanded unrestricted Claude access for mass surveillance and autonomous weapons; Anthropic refused and the DoD struck a deal with OpenAI instead. All Anthropic AI must be removed from military systems within **180 days**.

Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei issued a memo calling OpenAI's messaging **"straight up lies"** and accusing Sam Altman of falsely presenting himself as a peacemaker.

Apple announced a sweeping hardware refresh with AI at the center:
• **MacBook Air M5** — 2x faster AI image gen vs M4
• **MacBook Pro M5 Pro/Max** — 4x faster LLM processing vs M4 Pro
• **MacBook Neo** — affordable iPad-class chip laptop
• **iPhone 17e** and **iPad Air M4** also announced

M5 chips deliver up to **8x faster AI image generation** vs M1. Apple Intelligence integrates Google Gemini via Private Cloud Compute. Pre-orders open now; ships **March 11**.

items.text: **🇨🇳 3. China's Two Sessions Open with AI Dominance Agenda**

body.text: China's annual legislative sessions (全国两会) kicked off with AI as the defining policy theme. The **15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030)** locks in AI, chips, humanoid robotics, and clean energy as national priorities. The "AI+" initiative expands AI integration across all industrial sectors. Beijing is explicitly countering U.S. export controls by investing in domestic chip ecosystems (Huawei, Cambricon).

DeepSeek V4 — the most anticipated model of 2026 — has not yet launched but is imminent, timed around Two Sessions. Key specs: **1 trillion parameters** (32B active, MoE), fully multimodal (text/image/video), **1M-token context**, optimized for Huawei Ascend chips, projected at **$0.10–$0.30/M tokens** (up to 50x cheaper than GPT-5). Reportedly rivals Claude and GPT on long-context coding benchmarks.

items.text: **📰 5. Meta Signs $150M AI Content Deal with News Corp**

body.text: Meta signed a **3-year AI content licensing deal** with News Corp for up to **$50M/year** — covering Wall Street Journal, The Times, and other publications for AI training and chatbot responses. News Corp CEO Robert Thomson reframed the company as an **"AI input company."** Follows News Corp's 2024 $250M OpenAI deal. Crystallizes the emerging media business model: monetize archives as AI training data.

items.text: **🏛️ 6. U.S. AI Legislation Advances in Multiple States**

body.text: **New York** advanced two AI bills to third reading: S 6955 (AI Training Data Transparency) and S 933 (Chief AI Officer position). **Florida's AI Bill of Rights** approved by the Senate. Federal AI legislation remains quiet in 2026, leaving states to drive regulatory frameworks.

• **xAI Grok 4.20 Beta 2** — improved instruction-following, reduced hallucinations, better LaTeX typesetting
• **Samsung** targets 800M Gemini AI-equipped devices by end of 2026
• **MiniMax M2.5** reportedly rivals Claude Opus 4.6 at significantly lower cost

• **DeepSeek V4** — could drop any day; benchmark results vs GPT-5 and Claude incoming
• **Anthropic lawsuit** against Pentagon expected in coming days
• **China Two Sessions** policy details emerging over next week (through ~March 14)
• **Apple hardware** ships March 11; first real-world M5 AI performance data incoming
